children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a pilot program for integrated care during an episode of care provided to an applicable beneficiary around a hospitalization in order to improve the coordination, quality, and efficiency of health care services under this subchapter.

children.label: i
children.text: is entitled to, or enrolled for, benefits under part A and enrolled for benefits under part B of such subchapter, but not enrolled under part C or a PACE program under ; andsection 1395eee of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: is admitted to a hospital for an applicable condition.

children.label: i
children.text: Whether the conditions selected include a mix of chronic and acute conditions.

children.label: ii
children.text: Whether the conditions selected include a mix of surgical and medical conditions.

children.label: iii
children.text: Whether a condition is one for which there is evidence of an opportunity for providers of services and suppliers to improve the quality of care furnished while reducing total expenditures under this subchapter.

children.label: I
children.text: the number of readmissions; and

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of expenditures for post-acute care spending under this subchapter.

children.label: v
children.text: Whether a condition is high-volume and has high post-acute care expenditures under this subchapter.

children.label: vi
children.text: Which conditions the Secretary determines are most amenable to bundling across the spectrum of care given practice patterns under this subchapter.

children.label: i
children.text: Acute care inpatient services.

children.label: ii
children.text: Physicians’ services delivered in and outside of an acute care hospital setting.

children.label: iii
children.text: Outpatient hospital services, including emergency department services.

children.label: iv
children.text: Post-acute care services, including home health services, skilled nursing services, inpatient rehabilitation services, and inpatient hospital services furnished by a long-term care hospital.

children.label: v
children.text: Other services the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.label: I
children.text: the 3 days prior to the admission of the applicable beneficiary to a hospital for the applicable condition;

children.label: II
children.text: the length of stay of the applicable beneficiary in such hospital; and

children.label: III
children.text: the 30 days following the discharge of the applicable beneficiary from such hospital.

children.heading: Establishment of period by the Secretary
children.label: ii Establishment of period by the Secretary
children.text: The Secretary, as appropriate, may establish a period (other than the period described in clause (i)) for an episode of care under the pilot program.

children.heading: Physicians’ services
children.label: E Physicians’ services
children.text: The term “physicians’ services” has the meaning given such term in .section 1395x(q) of this title

children.heading: Pilot program
children.label: F Pilot program
children.text: The term “pilot program” means the pilot program under this section.

children.heading: Provider of services
children.label: G Provider of services
children.text: The term “provider of services” has the meaning given such term in .section 1395x(u) of this title

children.heading: Readmission
children.label: H Readmission
children.text: The term “readmission” has the meaning given such term in .section 1395ww(q)(5)(E) of this title

children.heading: Supplier
children.label: I Supplier
children.text: The term “supplier” has the meaning given such term in .section 1395x(d) of this title

children.heading: Deadline for implementation
children.label: 3 Deadline for implementation
children.text: The Secretary shall establish the pilot program not later than .January 1, 2013

children.heading: Determination of patient assessment instrument
children.label: 1 Determination of patient assessment instrument
children.text: The Secretary shall determine which patient assessment instrument (such as the Continuity Assessment Record and Evaluation (CARE) tool) shall be used under the pilot program to evaluate the applicable condition of an applicable beneficiary for purposes of determining the most clinically appropriate site for the provision of post-acute care to the applicable beneficiary.

children.heading: Development of quality measures for an episode of care and for post-acute care
children.label: 2 Development of quality measures for an episode of care and for post-acute care
i
children.text: for episodes of care; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for post-acute care.

children.heading: Site-neutral post-acute care quality measures
children.label: B Site-neutral post-acute care quality measures
children.text: Any quality measures developed under subparagraph (A)(ii) shall be site-neutral.

children.heading: Coordination with quality measure development and endorsement procedures
children.label: C Coordination with quality measure development and endorsement procedures
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that the development of quality measures under subparagraph (A) is done in a manner that is consistent with the measures developed and endorsed under section 1395aaa and 1395aaa–1 of this title that are applicable to all post-acute care settings.1So in original. Probably should be “sections”.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the pilot program shall be conducted for a period of 5 years.

children.label: I
children.text: reduce spending under this subchapter without reducing the quality of care; or

children.label: II
children.text: improve the quality of care and reduce spending;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Chief Actuary of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services certifies that such expansion would reduce program spending under this subchapter; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the Secretary determines that such expansion would not deny or limit the coverage or provision of benefits under this subchapter for individuals.

children.heading: Participating providers of services and suppliers
children.label: 2 Participating providers of services and suppliers

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An entity comprised of providers of services and suppliers, including a hospital, a physician group, a skilled nursing facility, and a home health agency, who are otherwise participating under this subchapter, may submit an application to the Secretary to provide applicable services to applicable individuals under this section.

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: B Requirements
children.text: The Secretary shall develop requirements for entities to participate in the pilot program under this section. Such requirements shall ensure that applicable beneficiaries have an adequate choice of providers of services and suppliers under the pilot program.

children.heading: Establishment of payment methods
children.label: i Establishment of payment methods
children.text: The Secretary shall develop payment methods for the pilot program for entities participating in the pilot program. Such payment methods may include bundled payments and bids from entities for episodes of care. The Secretary shall make payments to the entity for services covered under this section.

children.heading: No additional program expenditures
children.label: ii No additional program expenditures
children.text: Payments under this section for applicable items and services under this subchapter (including payment for services described in subparagraph (B)) for applicable beneficiaries for a year shall be established in a manner that does not result in spending more for such entity for such beneficiaries than would otherwise be expended for such entity for such beneficiaries for such year if the pilot program were not implemented, as estimated by the Secretary.

children.heading: Inclusion of certain services
children.label: B Inclusion of certain services
children.text: A payment methodology tested under the pilot program shall include payment for the furnishing of applicable services and other appropriate services, such as care coordination, medication reconciliation, discharge planning, transitional care services, and other patient-centered activities as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: be comprehensive, covering the costs of applicable services and other appropriate services furnished to an individual during an episode of care (as determined by the Secretary); and

children.label: II
children.text: be made to the entity which is participating in the pilot program.

children.heading: Requirement for provision of applicable services and other appropriate services
children.label: ii Requirement for provision of applicable services and other appropriate services
children.text: Applicable services and other appropriate services for which payment is made under this subparagraph shall be furnished or directed by the entity which is participating in the pilot program.

children.heading: Payment for post-acute care services after the episode of care
children.label: D Payment for post-acute care services after the episode of care
children.text: The Secretary shall establish procedures, in the case where an applicable beneficiary requires continued post-acute care services after the last day of the episode of care, under which payment for such services shall be made.

children.label: i
children.text: Functional status improvement.

children.label: ii
children.text: Reducing rates of avoidable hospital readmissions.

children.label: iii
children.text: Rates of discharge to the community.

children.label: iv
children.text: Rates of admission to an emergency room after a hospitalization.

children.label: v
children.text: Incidence of health care acquired infections.

children.label: vi
children.text: Efficiency measures.

children.label: vii
children.text: Measures of patient-centeredness of care.

children.label: viii
children.text: Measures of patient perception of care.

children.label: ix
children.text: Other measures, including measures of patient outcomes, determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A entity shall submit data to the Secretary on quality measures established under subparagraph (A) during each year of the pilot program (in a form and manner, subject to clause (iii), specified by the Secretary).

children.heading: Submission of data through electronic health record
children.label: ii Submission of data through electronic health record
children.text: To the extent practicable, the Secretary shall specify that data on measures be submitted under clause (i) through the use of an qualified electronic health record (as defined in ) in a manner specified by the Secretary.section 300jj(13) of this title

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: d Waiver
children.text: The Secretary may waive such provisions of this subchapter and subchapter XI as may be necessary to carry out the pilot program.

children.heading: Independent evaluation and reports on pilot program
children.label: e Independent evaluation and reports on pilot program
A
children.text: improved quality measures established under subsection (c)(4)(A);

children.label: B
children.text: improved health outcomes;

children.label: C
children.text: improved applicable beneficiary access to care; and

children.label: D
children.text: reduced spending under this subchapter.

children.heading: Interim report
children.label: A Interim report
children.text: Not later than 2 years after the implementation of the pilot program, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the initial results of the independent evaluation conducted under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Final report
children.label: B Final report
children.text: Not later than 3 years after the implementation of the pilot program, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the final results of the independent evaluation conducted under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: f Consultation
children.text: The Secretary shall consult with representatives of small rural hospitals, including critical access hospitals (as defined in ), regarding their participation in the pilot program. Such consultation shall include consideration of innovative methods of implementing bundled payments in hospitals described in the preceding sentence, taking into consideration any difficulties in doing so as a result of the low volume of services provided by such hospitals.section 1395x(mm)(1) of this title

children.heading: Application of pilot program to continuing care hospitals
children.label: g Application of pilot program to continuing care hospitals

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In conducting the pilot program, the Secretary shall apply the provisions of the program so as to separately pilot test the continuing care hospital model.

children.label: A
children.text: Such model shall be tested without the limitation to the conditions selected under subsection (a)(2)(B).

children.label: B
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (a)(2)(D), an episode of care shall be defined as the full period that a patient stays in the continuing care hospital plus the first 30 days following discharge from such hospital.

children.heading: Continuing care hospital defined
children.label: 3 Continuing care hospital defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “continuing care hospital” means an entity that has demonstrated the ability to meet patient care and patient safety standards and that provides under common management the medical and rehabilitation services provided in inpatient rehabilitation hospitals and units (as defined in ), long term care hospitals (as defined in section 1395ww(d)(1)(B)(iv)(I) of this title), and skilled nursing facilities (as defined in ) that are located in a hospital described in .section 1395ww(d)(1)(B)(ii) of this title2section 1395i–3(a) of this titlesection 1395ww(d) of this titleSee References in Text note below.2

children.heading: Administration
children.label: h Administration
children.text: Chapter 35 of title 44 shall not apply to the selection, testing, and evaluation of models or the expansion of such models under this section.
